title: Photon energy
short: The energy carried by a single quantum of light, E = h·f, measured in joules (J).
full:
Light comes in indivisible packets called photons, and each carries an energy proportional to its frequency, E = h·f, where h is Planck's constant. Higher-frequency light (blue, ultraviolet, X-rays) has more energetic photons than lower-frequency light (red, infrared).

This quantisation explains why ultraviolet light can damage skin while brighter red light cannot: a single photon must carry enough energy to do the job. Photon energies are often quoted in electronvolts (1 eV ≈ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ J).

The SI unit is the joule (J).
